screen-sharing.q: Will the teleprompter window show up on Google Meet screen sharing?
screen-sharing.a: No. VoicePrompter uses macOS system-level exclusions. When sharing your screen or browser window on Google Meet, the prompter window remains completely invisible to other participants.

extraFaqs.q: Do I need to install a Google Meet browser extension?
extraFaqs.a: No. VoicePrompter is a native macOS app. It runs completely independently, so you don't need to install any Chrome extensions, Safari add-ons, or modify your browser settings to use it with Google Meet.

extraFaqs.q: Can other Google Meet participants tell I am reading a script?
extraFaqs.a: No. Since you can drag and resize the transparent VoicePrompter window directly next to or below your Mac's webcam, your eye line remains completely natural. Other participants will see you looking directly at them while you read.

extraFaqs.q: How to use a teleprompter with Google Meet?
extraFaqs.a: Using VoicePrompter with Google Meet is simple: 1. Launch VoicePrompter on your Mac. 2. Load your script. 3. Position the transparent prompter window directly next to your webcam. 4. Share your screen or browser window in Google Meet—the prompter is automatically excluded and invisible to other participants. 5. Speak naturally, and the prompter will scroll in sync with your voice.

screen-sharing.q: Will the teleprompter window show up to other Zoom participants?
screen-sharing.a: No. When sharing your screen or a specific window in Zoom, the VoicePrompter window is automatically hidden at the system level. Other participants will only see your slides or screen, not the script.

extraFaqs.q: Do I need to authorize VoicePrompter in the Zoom App Marketplace?
extraFaqs.a: No. VoicePrompter is a standalone desktop application for macOS and does not connect to the Zoom API. There is no account authorization, integration, or Marketplace installation required.

extraFaqs.q: Will Zoom's screen recording capture the teleprompter window?
extraFaqs.a: No. Thanks to macOS native window exclusion APIs, the prompter window remains completely invisible to Zoom's local or cloud recording. Only you can see it on your screen.

extraFaqs.q: How to use a teleprompter with Zoom meetings?
extraFaqs.a: To use VoicePrompter with Zoom: 1. Launch the app on your Mac. 2. Load your script from clipboard. 3. Drag the transparent window next to your webcam. 4. Start your Zoom call. Even if you share your desktop or application window, the prompter is system-level hidden from participants. 5. Speak naturally as it auto-scrolls following your voice.

screen-sharing.q: Will the teleprompter window show up to other Teams participants?
screen-sharing.a: No. Just like with Zoom and Google Meet, macOS handles window exclusion at the system level. The prompter window remains completely invisible to others during screen sharing on Microsoft Teams.

extraFaqs.q: Does VoicePrompter work with both Microsoft Teams Web and the desktop app?
extraFaqs.a: Yes. Because VoicePrompter floats at the system level on macOS, it works flawlessly whether you use Microsoft Teams in Chrome/Safari, or run the native Teams desktop client.

extraFaqs.q: Will Teams participants see my prompter when I share my screen?
extraFaqs.a: No. When you share your desktop or a specific window in Microsoft Teams, macOS excludes the VoicePrompter window from the video feed. Other participants will only see your presentation—never the prompter.

extraFaqs.q: How to use a teleprompter with Microsoft Teams?
extraFaqs.a: To use VoicePrompter with Microsoft Teams: 1. Start the app on your macOS device. 2. Load your presentation notes. 3. Float the prompter window directly below your webcam. 4. Share your screen on Microsoft Teams—the prompter window is automatically hidden from the shared feed. 5. Present naturally as the script scrolls along with your speech.

screen-sharing.q: Will the teleprompter window show up on my Loom recordings?
screen-sharing.a: No. VoicePrompter is built to be system-level invisible during screen recording. When you record your screen using Loom, the prompter window will not appear on the finished video.

extraFaqs.q: Will the prompter window show up in my Loom recordings if I use the browser extension?
extraFaqs.a: No. VoicePrompter is fully integrated with macOS window capture exclusions. When you record your screen using Loom (via browser extension or the desktop app), the prompter window is automatically hidden from the output video.

extraFaqs.q: Can I use VoicePrompter for recording Loom demos offline?
extraFaqs.a: Yes. Since VoicePrompter runs entirely on your local Mac, you can load your script from clipboard and record your demos offline. The prompter will guide your speech while staying invisible in the final Loom upload.

extraFaqs.q: How to use a teleprompter with Loom video recordings?
extraFaqs.a: To use VoicePrompter with Loom: 1. Launch VoicePrompter. 2. Load your script. 3. Drag the prompter window close to your Mac camera. 4. Record your screen/camera with Loom. The prompter remains completely hidden on your recordings. 5. Present naturally without manual scrolling.

screen-sharing.q: Will the teleprompter window be captured by Riverside?
screen-sharing.a: No. Riverside captures the feed from your browser or screen, but macOS window exclusion ensures that the VoicePrompter window is completely invisible to all capture software, keeping your script private.

extraFaqs.q: Will VoicePrompter cause any audio echo or sync issues on Riverside?
extraFaqs.a: No. VoicePrompter's voice-activated scrolling relies on Apple's native speech recognition APIs which process audio locally. It does not output any audio feed or interfere with Riverside's high-quality audio recordings.

extraFaqs.q: Is my script content private on Riverside podcast recordings?
extraFaqs.a: Yes. Since Riverside recording happens via browser window or screen capture, macOS native window exclusion prevents the prompter from showing up on the Riverside feed. Your script, talking points, and cues remain 100% private to you.

extraFaqs.q: How to use a teleprompter with Riverside podcast recordings?
extraFaqs.a: To use VoicePrompter on Riverside: 1. Start VoicePrompter. 2. Load your podcast notes. 3. Place the transparent window next to your webcam. 4. Record your feed on Riverside. The prompter window is excluded from Riverside's capture. 5. Talk naturally as it follows your voice.
